meta.reportLabel: Fri Report — WAR PREMIUM FADES: WTI slips a 2nd session to ~$72 (Brent ~$77) DESPITE live strikes as the US campaign is read as degrading Iran's shipping-attack capability + glut caps rallies; gold floors ~$4,075, silver ~$57.7. USD stays firm (SEP 1-2 hikes, zero cuts). CAD oil floor eroding into today's Canada jobs (~+10k) - short EUR/USD, long USD/CAD; USOIL downgraded to a fading long, metals RANGE; Jul 14 CPI the arbiter.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The kinetic escalation is still live but the market has stopped paying up for it: WTI fell a second session to ~$72 (Brent ~$77) DESPITE CENTCOM's second round of strikes, because the US campaign is read as degrading Iran's capacity to attack shipping (supply-protective) and the stacked-crude glut caps rallies. That partial normalization let gold find a floor at ~$4,075 (off Wed's ~$4,030 low) and yields steady, while the USD stays firm on the SEP's 1-2-hike guidance. With the CAD oil floor eroding into today's Canada jobs, the cleanest expressions flip back to the USD legs. Everything headline-hostage into the Jul 14 CPI and the post-funeral Doha reconvene.
meta.nextBigEvent: Tue Jul 14, 12:30 UTC: US June CPI - the 4.2%-regime check that decides the Sept hike (~50%); oil fading lowers the bar for a soft print. Canada June jobs today 12:30 UTC is the nearer catalyst.

dailyRead: The story today is that the war premium is fading even as the strikes continue: WTI slipped a second session to ~$72 (Brent ~$77) despite CENTCOM's second consecutive round of strikes overnight (~80 Iranian targets, 60+ IRGC boats), because the market now reads the US campaign as degrading Iran's ability to hit shipping - supply-protective - and the stacked-crude glut keeps capping rallies. That partial normalization let gold find a floor at ~$4,075 (off Wednesday's ~$4,030 low) while silver stayed pinned near ~$57.7 (Dec-2025 lows, ratio ~67). The USD stays firm on the higher-for-longer read (Fed SEP now shows 1-2 hikes in 2026, zero cuts), and with the oil floor under CAD eroding just as Canada's June jobs print today (12:30 UTC, consensus ~+10k after May's +88k), the cleanest expression flips back to the USD legs: short EUR/USD and long USD/CAD, with USOIL downgraded to a fading long and the metals still RANGE. Everything remains headline-hostage into the Jul 14 US CPI and the post-funeral Doha reconvene.

today.headline: Oil floor ERODING (WTI ~$72) into today's June jobs (~+10k)
today.read: The oil bid that firmed the floor under the board's weakest currency is fading as WTI slips a second session, just as June jobs (12:30 UTC) are expected to calm to ~+10k after May's +88k. Both point the same way - USD/CAD stays a long, but a jobs beat or an oil re-spike flips the setup.

today.headline: Gold finds a floor ~$4,075 as the oil-yield pressure eases
today.read: Off Wednesday's ~$4,030 low: the metals selloff paused as oil faded and yields steadied, but it stays RANGE - only a ceasefire that drops yields while keeping risk-off bid revives the long; a hot CPI re-arms the selloff.

today.headline: Firm but the energy-CPI push eases as oil fades a 2nd day
today.read: The SEP now pencils 1-2 hikes and zero cuts, keeping the higher-for-longer bid, but two down sessions in oil soften the energy pass-through into June CPI - a soft Jul 14 print would crack the story fast. DXY grinds ~101.

today.headline: Yield pin eases marginally but spot holds ~162
today.read: Fading oil relaxes the rising-yield prop a touch, yet spot stays ~162 in the MoF zone (Y11.73tn precedent) with no intervention - the strength case is still the backstop asymmetry, not momentum. A yields re-spike to 163+ forces the MoF's hand.

today.headline: Premium faded to ~$72 DESPITE live strikes - long downgraded
today.read: The US campaign is read as degrading Iran's shipping-attack capability and the glut caps rallies, so momentum turned lower; the tail (Hormuz, ongoing strikes) keeps a long bias but the edge thinned. Headline-hostage - size small.

today.headline: No change — soft with the core wrinkle; the firm-USD tape does the work
today.read: Flash 2.8% cooling still guts Jul 23 urgency, but core 2.5% keeps the Sept hike alive - EUR/USD short is the clean USD-leg expression. The squeeze risk is a hawkish Jul 23 tone.

today.headline: No change — firm and quiet; fading political risk adds a mild bid
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, and post-Starmer political calm supports GBP/USD >1.34. Firm but rangebound vs a firm high-yield USD; long tilt vs AUD.

strength.tag: SEP 1-2 hikes, zero cuts; oil fading eases the energy push
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2% on participation 61.5%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, SEP now pencils 1-2 hikes and zero 2026 cuts, Sept ~50%. Oil fading two sessions eases the energy-CPI floor at the margin.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; the SEP's 1-2-hike guidance keeps the Sept tail alive, but two down sessions in oil soften the energy pass-through. Jun CPI (Jul 14) is the arbiter. Risk: a second soft CPI turns the NFP crack into a trend. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; fading political risk adds a bid
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); post-Starmer political calm supports GBP/USD >1.34.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round; fading political risk adds a mild bid. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: 2.50%, first hike since 2023; higher hurdle next
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0 - but the statement was the dovish version: headline inflation now seen PEAKED at 3.9% in Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move. NZD popped and faded; soft China CPI (+1%) trims the growth story.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hike delivered but guidance capped it - Sept still likely, not aggressively priced; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: a 4%+ CPI print re-arms the hawks; risk-off beta cuts the other way. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: spot ~162; yield pin eases as oil fades, MoF still the story
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Oil fading eases the rising-yield prop slightly but spot still ~162 - deep in the proven MoF zone (Y11.73tn/$74bn precedent) with no intervention yet.
strength.forward: 🦅 The haven bid is real and the yield prop is easing marginally, but spot holds ~162 - the JPY-strength case is still the backstop asymmetry more than momentum; BoJ Jul 31 stays hawkish. Risk: a yields re-spike to 163+ forces the MoF's hand; fast de-escalation restores the carry grind. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.tag: thin Aug tail (~36%), China CPI +1% demand pulse light
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); the Aug 11 hike was priced only ~36% before the escalation. China CPI missed at +1%, trimming the demand pulse.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The sticky-core floor stands but risk-off + a soft China pulse hit the beta first - an oil-shock growth scare argues RBA patience through Q3. Risk: a durable de-escalation plus a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) flips it straight back to hawkish. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core 2.5%; firm-USD tape
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting; the firm-USD tape does the work on EUR/USD.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected) but rising core keeps a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on core squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: WTI ~$72 - oil floor eroding; jobs today ~+10k
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; May +88k (June jobs today 12:30 UTC, consensus ~+10k); wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold. WTI slipping to ~$72 is eroding the floor under the board's weakest currency.
strength.forward: 🔻 Still the softest macro on the board and the oil floor is now eroding as WTI fades; BoC sidelined Jul 15 (core ~2% vs energy headline); today's June jobs (~+10k exp) is the domestic catalyst. Risk: an oil re-spike or a jobs beat firms it back. Next BoC Jul 15.

symbols.risk: The core-2.5% wrinkle turns Jul 23 hawkish; a soft US CPI Jul 14 fades the USD bid.

symbols.risk: An oil re-spike or a jobs beat firms CAD; soft US CPI Jul 14.

symbols.risk: A Doha/'capability degraded' narrative crushes it; a tanker/Hormuz shock re-arms it.

symbols.risk: Yields re-spike and drag it to 163+; de-escalation restores carry.

symbols.why: Oil fade weakens CAD, yields pin JPY - no clean vector.

symbols.why: Fading oil eases the CAD headwind while risk-off + soft China hit AUD - both legs blunted.

symbols.why: Dovish-hike NZD vs an oil-softening CAD; both catalysts spent.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a yield-pinned JPY - mild long tilt as oil-yield pressure eases, but no momentum.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: hawkish BoE (services 3.7%, fading political risk) vs a firm hawkish-hold Fed - no clean edge.

symbols.risk: The AU Q2 CPI late July sets direction.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs risk-hit AUD - long tilt, but stretched and headline-hostage.
symbols.risk: De-escalation + a hot AU CPI reverses the tilt.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a delivered-but-dovish RBNZ - no vector post-event.
symbols.risk: NZ Q2 CPI; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: Firm long tilt: the SEP's 1-2-hike read and soft EUR (57.6% weight) hold DXY ~101, but Sept is only ~50% and oil fading eases the yield push - it grinds, not breaks. Jul 14 CPI is the trigger.
symbols.risk: De-escalation + a soft CPI Jul 14 unwind the bid.

symbols.why: The haven bid is intact but yields still pin JPY at ~162 with no intervention - a backstop-asymmetry play, not a momentum long. (Rises when JPY strengthens - opposite ticket to XXX/JPY.)
symbols.risk: A yields re-spike drags it lower and forces the MoF; de-escalation caps it.

symbols.risk: A deeper oil spike turns it SHORT; a clean de-escalation restores the melt-up.

symbols.why: Gold found a floor at ~$4,075 (off Wed's ~$4,030 low) as the oil-yield pressure eased and the escalation-selling paused; still range-bound until yields and the Fed path settle.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire that drops yields AND keeps risk-off bid revives the long; a hot CPI re-arms the selloff.

symbols.why: Silver stays pinned near ~$57.7 (Dec-2025 lows, ratio ~67) - the industrial leg plus real yields keep it heavier than gold even as the metals steady.

catalysts.note: Consensus ~+10k, unemp 6.6% - whether May's +88k was noise; feeds a CAD whose oil floor is now fading, ahead of BoC Jul 15.
Confirmation print; EZ inflation texture into ECB Jul 23.
Technical round concluded with 'positive progress'; the next sit is the first hard de-escalation signal - oil, CAD, JPY, gold all key off it.
The 4.2%-regime check - re-arms or kills the Sept hike (~50%); oil fading lowers the bar for a soft print.
Hold; the fading oil floor vs core ~2% and a technical recession.
RBNZ says headline PEAKED at 3.9% - the print validates or breaks the 'higher hurdle' guidance.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Hold; core-2.5% is the Sept-hike signal.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism; Sept ~50% hangs on the Jul 14 CPI.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162 and the MoF zone hot; oil fading eases the pin slightly.
Iran: fees 'definitely'; Washington: rejected - now with active strikes in between.
3 vessels hit, CENTCOM strikes ongoing, sanctions reimposed; every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold.

geopolitics: The kinetic escalation is still live but the market has stopped paying up for it. Overnight CENTCOM ran a second consecutive round of strikes - following the previous night's hit on ~80 Iranian targets including 60+ IRGC boats - and Treasury's reimposed oil sanctions stayed in force, yet WTI fell a second session to ~$72 (Brent ~$77) and tanker traffic through Hormuz stayed thin (13 crossings Wednesday vs a 33/day norm). Two things flipped the read: the US campaign is increasingly interpreted as degrading Iran's capacity to attack shipping (supply-protective rather than supply-threatening), and the stacked-crude glut (Iranian barrels plus Saudi ~90% output) caps every rally. The Doha technical track remains alive - 'positive progress' reported, reconvene after Khamenei's funeral - even as Trump calls the June-17 MoU 'over' and ~6,000 seafarers stay stranded. With the oil-inflation impulse easing, the RATES channel that smashed metals on Wednesday relaxed: gold found a floor at ~$4,075 and yields steadied, though the USD stays firm on the SEP's 1-2-hike guidance. Transmission by currency: USD still the cleanest beneficiary (higher-for-longer holds even as the energy push fades); CAD loses its oil floor just as June jobs are expected to soften today, firming the USD/CAD long; JPY's pin eases marginally but spot holds ~162 (MoF backstop still the story); AUD and NZD wear risk-off beta plus the soft China pulse (CPI +1%); EUR stays soft on the firm-USD tape; GER40 gets a small tailwind back from the oil pullback. The two-way risk is unchanged and large: a Doha breakthrough would crush what premium remains, while a genuine Hormuz-closure or tanker shock re-arms the whole complex overnight - which is why the board still carries no high-conviction pairs and the oil-linked calls are sized small.
